UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF ALLYSYN OVERTON'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND MODIFICATION OF ORDER RE: MOTION TO COMPLY, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO UNFILE AND SEAL RECORDSIS DENIED AS FOLLOWS. MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED AS CCP 1008 IS NOT APPLICABLE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES AS MOVING PARTY SEEKS THE ORDER OF THE COURT TO BE CONFORMED TO WHAT THE MOVING PARTY BELIEVES WAS THE COURT'S ORAL RULING. MOTION TO SEAL IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR PLAINTIFF TO RE-FILE IT ON PROPER NOTICE AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 2.550 AND 2.551. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE LILLIAN SING, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, CROSS COMPLAINANT PATRICK LAKE'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM AND TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DISMISSAL PURSUANT TO CCP 473 IS GRANTED BASED ON MANDATORY PROVISION OF CCP 473(B). MARCH 20, 2014 ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IS VACATED AND THE NEW ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND SHALL BE ENTERED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT SHALL HAVE UNTIL JUNE 20, 2014 TO FILE AND SERVE AN AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S COUNSEL SHALL PAY $550 TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY THE ORDER. MOVING PARTY SHALL PRESENT THE ORDER GRANTING THIS MOTION AND THE ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY. CLERK: NANCY BAUTISTA. NOT REPORTED (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Meaghan Zore (415-347-0004) for Plaintiff/Cross Defendant and Moving party Allysyn Overton
Jonathan Bornstein of Bornstein & Bornstein (415-409-7611) for Defendant and opposing party Patrick Lake.

Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: CROSS-DEFENDANT ALLYSYN OVERTON'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Cross-Complainant's TACC is stricken. Cross-Complainant shall file an amended cross-complaint for breach of 2011 Lease only no later than December 1, 2014 by 4:00 p.m. Motion to seal is granted. Cross-Complainant shall prepare a copy of August 26, 2014 opposition with exhibits under seal and submit it to the Record's Department no later that December 1, 2014 by 4:00 p.m. Request for sanctions is denied. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Continued To Oct-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2014 Set For Case Management Conference On Dec-17-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of And Motion For Reconsideration And Modification Of Order Re: Motion To Comply, Or In The Alternative To Unfile And Seal Records
Off Calendar Oct-09-2014 Continued To Oct-10-14 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 501

Notice Of And Motion For Reconsideration And Modification Of Order Re: Motion To Comply, Or In The Alternative To Unfile And Seal Records
Off Calendar Oct-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF ALLYSYN OVERTON's Motion For Reconsideration And Modification Of Order Re: Motion To Comply, Or In The Alternative To Unfile And Seal Recordsis denied as follows. Motion for reconsideration is denied as CCP 1008 is not applicable under the circumstances as moving party seeks the order of the court to be conformed to what the moving party believes was the Court's oral ruling. Motion to seal is denied without prejudice for Plaintiff to re-file it on proper notice and in compliance with CRC 2.550 and 2.551. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Lillian Sing, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Meaghan Zore, Esq. of Zore Law (415-347-0004), counsel for plaintiff/cross-defendant/demurring party
Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein (415-409-7611), counsel for defendant/cross-complainant/opposing party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALLYSYN OVERTON'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED is sustained with leave to amend to attach 2011 contract as requested in the late-filed opposition. Demurer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant's late-filed opposition is stricken. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Requests, And For An Order Deeming The Matters Admitted In Defts Request For Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Meaghan Zore, Esq. of Zore Law, (415-347-0004), for Allysyn Overton, Plaintiff and moving party.
Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein, (415-409-7611), for Patrick Lake, Defendant and opposing party.

Court hears arguments on the notice of motion not received by Defendant's counsel as claimed by Mr. Bronstein, the timeliness of filing of defendant's opposition, the defendant's responses to Discovery not verified and sanctions being sought by plaintiff.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: PLAINTIFF ALLYSYN OVERTON's Motion To Compel Discovery Requests is GRANTED. Responses received by Plaintiff are found by Court not verified. Sanctions in favor of the Plaintiff is granted in the amount of 1,000.00. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Lillian K. Sing, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Continued To Sep-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF ALLYSYN OVERTON's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to September 3, 2014. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide a courtesy copy of the operative Cross-Complaint to Department 501 no later than August 8, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Moiton For Relief From And To Set Aside Entry Of Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473;
Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Apperances: Atty. Jonathan Bornstein (415-409-7611) appearing for Defendant and moving party./ No other apperances.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS COMPLAINANT PATRICK LAKE'S Motion For Relief From And To Set Aside Entry Of Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473 is GRANTED based on mandatory provision of CCP 473(b). March 20, 2014 Order sustaining demurer without leave to amend is vacated and the new order sustaining the demurer with leave to amend shall be entered. Cross-complainant shall have until June 20, 2014 to file and serve an amended cross-complaint. Cross-complainant's counsel shall pay $550 to Plaintiff's counsel within 10 days of entry the order. Moving party shall present the order granting this motion and the order sustaining demurer with leave to amend to the Court at the hearing. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Not Reported (D501)

Notice Of Moiton For Relief From And To Set Aside Entry Of Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473;
